channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: El director ejecutivo de McDonald’s probó una de sus hamburguesas. Técnicamente
item.description: Cuando Chris Kempczinski publicó un video de sí mismo comiendo, lo que llamó la atención no fue el producto que promocionaba. Fue cómo se lo comía.
item.dc:creator: Victor Mather

item.title: Word of the Day: fortitude
item.description: This word has appeared in 200 articles on NYTimes.com in the past year. Can you use it in a sentence?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: Late Night Isn’t Sad to See Kristi Noem Leave Homeland Security
item.description: “Sounds like someone’s about to become the FIFA secretary of homeland security,” Stephen Colbert joked.
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix
item.category: The Daily Show with Jon Stewart (TV Program)

item.title: 3 Australians Were on U.S. Submarine that Sank an Iranian Warship
item.description: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese’s disclosure about the attack in the Indian Ocean prompted concern that Australia’s close military cooperation with the United States could draw it into the fighting in the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Victoria Kim
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: An injured Iranian sailor is moved on a stretcher at Galle National Hospital in Sri Lanka after the torpedoing of the Iranian naval ship IRIS Dena on Thursday.

item.title: Live Updates: Tehran, Beirut and Tel Aviv Are Targeted in Attacks and Counterattacks
item.description: The Israeli military said it was striking Iran’s capital while pounding Hezbollah targets in Lebanon, as fighting there intensified. Iran said it was targeting Tel Aviv, the country’s state news agency reported.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

media:content.media:credit: Diego Ibarra Sanchez for The New York Times

item.title: ¿Tener un perro alarga la vida?
item.description: Las investigaciones sugieren que las mascotas pueden ayudar a mantenerte más sano, pero hay algunas advertencias.
item.dc:creator: Dana G. Smith

media:content.media:credit: Tony Luong para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Gracias a su perro, Rumi, Dhruv Kazi dice que pasa más tiempo al aire libre y ha llegado a conocer a sus vecinos y a los perros de estos.

item.title: America Won’t Save Iran
item.description: The most likely outcome of the American-Israeli bombing campaign is not the advent of liberal democracy but something worse.
item.dc:creator: Reza Aslan
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Photo illustration by The New York Times; source photograph by Atta Kenare/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images

item.title: As Trump Out-Putins Putin, Russia’s Global Influence Erodes
item.description: The conflict in Iran may give Moscow a short-term boost economically and in Ukraine. But it has also shown the limits of Russia’s partnerships.
item.dc:creator: Paul Sonne
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: President Vladimir V. Putin of Russia went to a wreath-laying ceremony at the Tomb of the Unknown Soldier in Moscow last month.

item.title: Jamil Jivani, a Friend of JD Vance, Channels Charlie Kirk With Young Men
item.description: Jamil Jivani, a Conservative rookie member of Parliament, follows an American playbook to win over young men on college campuses.
item.dc:creator: Norimitsu Onishi

media:content.media:credit: Gavin John for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Jamil Jivani, a conservative member of Parliament, talking with supporters after speaking at the University of Calgary last month.

item.title: Corrections: March 6, 2026
item.description: Corrections that appeared in print on Friday, March 6, 2026.

item.title: A Power Outage Made Us Possible
item.description: If he couldn’t see me or my place, maybe he wouldn’t judge me either.
item.dc:creator: William J. O’Brien

item.title: He Grew Up Watching Her on TV. Years Later, She Joined His World.
item.description: Brian Thalman became familiar with Brooke Hyland while watching her on “Dance Moms.” The two met seven years after her final season on the reality TV show.
item.dc:creator: Sadiba Hasan

item.title: They Met in a Chemistry Class, but the Connection Came Later
item.description: Jordan Goldberg and Adam Rossman were friends at the University of Texas at Austin. Years later, after building a life in New York, they married in Austin.
item.dc:creator: Donna Kim

item.title: On the Ninth Hole, Her Dad Took a Swing at Playing Cupid
item.description: Taylor Ross’s father asked Noah Koeppel, his new golf buddy’s son, if he wanted to meet his daughter. He was game. She wasn’t.
item.dc:creator: Rosalie R. Radomsky

item.title: Carolyn Bessette Kennedy’s Wedding Slip Dress Continues to Shape Modern Bridal Fashion
item.description: Thirty years after Ms. Bessette’s wedding to John F. Kennedy Jr., the FX series “Love Story” is renewing interest, again, in her minimalist style.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Lyon

item.title: He Had a Purple Heart, PTSD and a Rap Sheet. He Had to Leave the U.S.
item.description: After a long battle with drug addiction, Sae Joon Park felt settled in a new life. But he was deported last year and is now fighting to get back to Hawaii.
item.dc:creator: Max Kim

media:content.media:credit: Jean Chung for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Sae Joon Park in his apartment in Busan, South Korea, in February. Despite living in the U.S. for nearly 50 years, Park was ordered to move back to South Korea in June 2025.

item.title: The Good Times Are Over in China’s Liquor Capital
item.description: Once celebrated for its fiery spirits, the town of Maotai has reeled from a bad Chinese economy, changing tastes and a crackdown on boozy official banquets.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Higgins and Gilles Sabrié

media:content.media:description: Tanks of fermented sorghum at Jinjiao Distillery in Maotai, China, in February.

item.title: Their Favorite Projects? Inventing Cocktails and Card Games.
item.description: For Jai Lennard, a photographer, and Alelli Tanghal, an art director, collaboration has long been the foundation of their romance.
item.dc:creator: Valeriya Safronova

item.title: Quote of the Day: How To Marry Dance With Wit
item.description: Quotation of the Day for Friday, February 6, 2026.

item.title: D.C. Court Strikes Down Local Ban on High-Capacity Gun Magazines
item.description: A three-judge panel reversed the conviction of a man charged with possessing a 30-bullet magazine, saying it had constitutional protection.
item.dc:creator: Mattathias Schwartz

media:content.media:description: A semi-automatic hand gun with a 10 shot magazine, left, and a 15 shot magazine, right, at a gun store in California.

item.title: Representative Tony Gonzales Will Not Seek Re-election
item.description: Several House members had called for Mr. Gonzales, a Texas Republican, to step down after texts emerged showing his pursuit of a staff member who later killed herself.
item.dc:creator: J. David Goodman

media:content.media:description: Representative Tony Gonzales at a House Homeland Security Committee hearing in Washington last month.

item.title: U.S. Restarts Diplomatic Relations With Venezuela
item.description: Washington has rapidly warmed up ties with Venezuela — and applied major pressure on it — since capturing the country’s president, Nicolás Maduro, two months ago.
item.dc:creator: Max Bearak

media:content.media:credit: Adriana Loureiro Fernandez for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A protest last month in Caracas, Venezuela’s capital, demanding freedom for political prisoners.

item.title: ‘The Pitt’ Season 2, Episode 9 Recap: Family Separation
item.description: A young boy arrives at the emergency room this week with a firework-related injury, but his troubles run much deeper than that.
item.dc:creator: Sean T. Collins

media:content.media:description: From left, Sasha Compere, Noah Wyle and Isa Briones in “The Pitt.” Episode 9 included a story line in which a patient’s parents were seized during an immigration hearing and deported to Haiti.

item.title: NYT Crossword Answers for March 6, 2026
item.description: Adrian Johnson opens our solving weekend.
item.dc:creator: Sam Corbin

item.description: Lighting flashed in the sky as cars drove along a flooded Interstate 45 in Hutchins, Texas, on Wednesday.

item.title: Wallace Shawn’s ‘What We Did Before Our Moth Days’ Is Purgatory Done Right
item.description: The playwright and his collaborator André Gregory are together again, delivering a sumptuous set of interlinked monologues about life, death and betrayal.
item.dc:creator: Helen Shaw
item.category: What We Did Before Our Moth Days (Play)

media:content.media:description: From left, Hope Davis, John Early, Josh Hamilton and Maria Dizzia in “What We Did Before Our Moth Days” at the Greenwich House Theater in Manhattan.

item.title: DOJ Releases Missing Interviews With Woman Who Made Claims Against Trump in Epstein Files
item.description: The pages had been withheld from the trove of documents related to the convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein because of what officials called a mistaken determination that they were duplicates.
item.dc:creator: Devlin Barrett

media:content.media:credit: Michael A. McCoy for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The Justice Department acknowledged on Thursday that it had also identified about a dozen other documents that were “incorrectly coded as duplicative.”

item.title: Timeline: The Rise and Fall of Kristi Noem as Trump’s DHS Secretary
item.description: The homeland security secretary, who was fired by President Trump Thursday, helped fulfill his border pledges, but also drew negative attention to his administration.
item.dc:creator: Zolan Kanno-Youngs and Hamed Aleaziz

media:content.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Ms. Noem testified on Capitol Hill this week.

item.title: What Kristi Noem Should Do After President Trump Fired Her
item.description: It’s hard out there for a MAGA woman.
item.dc:creator: Michelle Cottle

item.title: Panic in Beirut After Israel Warns Residents to Evacuate
item.description: The streets of Beirut erupted in chaos on Thursday after Israel told people in the southern suburbs to evacuate. The Israeli military has been carrying out airstrikes in Dahiya, a Hezbollah stronghold, since the militant group fired rockets into northern Israel earlier this week.
item.dc:creator: Jorge Mitssunaga

media:content.media:description: Jammed roads in Beirut, the capital of Lebanon, after Israel’s military issued evacuation orders of entire neighborhood on Thursday.

item.title: What Are We Fighting For?
item.description: After launching a war in Iran, President Trump owes the American people a clear explanation of his objectives and possible endgame — something we have yet to receive, argues the Times Opinion writer W.J. Hennigan.
item.dc:creator: W.J. Hennigan and Stephanie Shen

item.title: India Is Turning Back to Russian Oil
item.description: India’s trade deal with President Trump was supposed to end its imports of oil from Russia. But then the conflict in the Middle East has cut off alternative supplies.
item.dc:creator: Alex Travelli
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: A Russian tanker in 2024 navigating the Bosporus, one route used to export oil to buyers abroad.

item.title: Minnesota Governor Walz and Minneapolis Mayor Welcome Kristi Noem’s Firing
item.description: Mayor Jacob Frey of Minneapolis said “Good riddance” after Ms. Noem’s ouster. Gov. Tim Walz and others said they still wanted a “complete overhaul” of the department.
item.dc:creator: Pooja Salhotra and Ernesto Londoño

media:content.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Gov. Tim Walz said Kristi Noem had done “a stunning amount of damage” during her tenure as homeland security secretary.

item.title: Israel Pounds Southern Beirut, a Hezbollah Stronghold
item.description: The strikes were the most intense bombardment since a cease-fire deal in late 2024.
item.dc:creator: Dayana Iwaza, Euan Ward, Hwaida Saad and Ephrat Livni

media:content.media:description: Smoke rising after an Israeli airstrike in the southern suburbs of Beirut, Lebanon, on early Friday.

item.title: State Department Is Accused of Slow Assistance in Iran War for Americans Abroad
item.description: Many veteran diplomats faulted the State Department for its response after the attacks in Iran began, and for its actions beforehand.
item.dc:creator: Michael Crowley and Edward Wong
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: Passengers evacuated from the Middle East arrived at Dulles International Airport on Thursday.

item.title: Iran War Pulls New Countries Into Conflict
item.description: The conflict between Iran, Israel and the United States expanded on Thursday after multiple Iranian attacks pushed leaders in Britain and Italy to accelerate their deployment of military assets to the region.
item.dc:creator: Meg Felling
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Ex-Executive at Blood Filter Start-Up Is Charged With Covering Up Deaths
item.description: ExThera Medical attracted cancer patients to Antigua with the promise that its devices could cure them. Its former chief regulatory officer faces up to three years in prison.
item.dc:creator: John Carreyrou

media:content.media:credit: Amir Hamja for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The clinic in Antigua where cancer patients were treated with ExThera’s blood filter.

item.title: Steve Daines’s Switcheroo Starts New Fight in Montana Senate Race
item.description: Minutes before the Senate filing deadline, Senator Steve Daines withdrew his re-election bid and an ally jumped in. Even some fellow Republicans criticized the 11th-hour switch.
item.dc:creator: Kellen Browning

media:content.media:credit: Thom Bridge/Independent Record, via Associated Press
media:content.media:description: Kurt Alme, who has been serving as the U.S. attorney for Montana, was endorsed by Senator Steve Daines and President Trump to succeed Mr. Daines in a race for what will now be an open seat.

item.title: Not Just Oil: In Venezuela, U.S. Interior Secretary Pushes for Mining Access
item.description: Venezuela is home to large reserves of rare earths, gold and other valuable minerals that the Trump administration wants to exert more control over.
item.dc:creator: Max Bearak and Lisa Friedman

media:content.media:description: Interior Secretary Doug Burgum and Delcy Rodríguez, Venezuela’s new leader, in Caracas on Wednesday.

item.title: Superfood Fuels Mating Frenzy for Critically Endangered Kakapo
item.description: Kakapos, which are reclusive and flightless and can live as long as humans, are found only in New Zealand. They feed on the fruit of the rimu tree.
item.dc:creator: Neil Vigdor

media:content.media:credit: Andrew Digby/Dept. of Conservation, New Zealand, via Associated Press
media:content.media:description: A kakapo with eggs on Anchor Island, in New Zealand. The island is known for lacking the kinds of predators that threaten the parrots.

item.title: Two Schools in Iran Damaged in U.S.-Israeli Bombing
item.description: Photos show that both schools, southeast of Tehran, sustained damage. No injuries were reported.
item.dc:creator: Christiaan Triebert and Parin Behrooz
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: The aftermath of a strike in Tehran, the Iranian capital, on Thursday.

item.title: Protection for Section 8 Renters Is Struck Down by N.Y. Appeals Court
item.description: A panel of appeals judges ruled that a state law banning housing discrimination against renters who use federal vouchers was unconstitutional.
item.dc:creator: Mihir Zaveri

media:content.media:description: More than 245,000 households across New York State use federal vouchers to pay rent.

item.title: President Fires Noem as Homeland Security Secretary
item.description: President Trump fired Kristi Noem, his embattled homeland security secretary, on Thursday and announced his plans to replace her with Senator Markwayne Mullin of Oklahoma.
item.dc:creator: Jackeline Luna

item.title: Senator Blumenthal Pushes to Investigate Kristi Noem for Perjury
item.description: Senator Richard Blumenthal of Connecticut said Democrats had evidence to suggest Kristi Noem lied under oath during a Senate hearing on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Carl Hulse and Michael Gold

media:content.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Senator Richard Blumenthal speaking on Capitol Hill in January. Blumenthal said that Kristi Noem’s removal did not protect her from an investigation.

item.title: Fewer Pregnant Women Received Acetaminophen in E.R.s After White House Warnings
item.description: A new analysis of prescription data found that emergency room orders for acetaminophen for pregnant women fell for weeks after the federal warning.
item.dc:creator: Azeen Ghorayshi

media:content.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: President Trump made an announcement about autism and Tylenol alongside Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. in the Roosevelt Room of the White House in Washington last September.

item.title: Post Iran: Vance vs. Carlson in 2028?
item.description: The war in Iran may have a lasting impact on the political careers of Trump’s closest allies. On “Interesting Times,” Curt Mills, the executive director of the American Conservative, shares his thoughts on how Trump’s interventionist policies might play out in the 2028 Republican primaries.
item.dc:creator: Interesting Times

item.title: Trump ‘Can’t Say No to Israel’
item.description: Who is calling the shots in the war with Iran? Curt Mills, the executive director of the American Conservative, thinks Israeli influence is guiding Trump’s decision to go to war in the Middle East. He explains why on this episode of “Interesting Times.”
item.dc:creator: Interesting Times
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: S.E.C. Settles Its Case Against Justin Sun, an Investor in the Trumps’ Crypto Coins
item.description: The decision to settle the court action is particularly striking because the defendant was accused of a serious violation of securities laws. He denied the charges.
item.dc:creator: Sharon LaFraniere and David Yaffe-Bellany

media:content.media:credit: Jason Andrew for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Justin Sun, founder of the crypto platform TRON, on his way to the White House for a private tour last year. Mr. Sun threw Mr. Trump’s fledgling crypto company a cash lifeline at a time when it was struggling.

item.title: Trump Pick Of Mullin For D.H.S. Sets Off Political Scramble in Oklahoma
item.description: The president’s choice of Senator Markwayne Mullin of Oklahoma to replace her at the Department of Homeland Security could trigger a cascade of aspirants for his seat.
item.dc:creator: Reid J. Epstein

media:content.media:credit: Al Drago for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Senator Markwayne Mullin of Oklahoma’s term expires at the end of this year, so whoever is appointed to fill his seat will be a short termer in the Senate.

item.title: Israel Attacks Beirut and Tehran as Fighting in Middle East Accelerates
item.description: The Israeli military said it had begun a “broad-scale” attack in Iran’s capital, hours after hitting Hezbollah strongholds in Lebanon. The Pentagon said Iran’s retaliatory missile strikes were decreasing.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

media:content.media:credit: Diego Ibarra Sanchez for The New York Times
